# A tour of Hacker News

*A guided tour of news.ycombinator.com — the front page, then a story's discussion thread.*

**URL:** https://news.ycombinator.com/

## Contents

1. [Scene 1: The front page](#1-scene-1-the-front-page)
2. [Scene 2: Inside the discussion](#2-scene-2-inside-the-discussion)

---

## 1. Scene 1: The front page <a id="1-scene-1-the-front-page"></a>

### 1.1 Land on the front page

Hacker News — the developer community's front page since 2007.

### 1.2 The top story

At the top sits the story with the most points right now — title, then a link out to its source.

1. Look at **the top story's title link**.
2. Notice **the top story's title link**.

### 1.3 Source domain

In parentheses next to the title: the source domain, so you know where you'd land before clicking.

1. Look at **the source domain badge**.
2. Notice **the source domain in parentheses**.

### 1.4 Vote arrow

The little up-arrow on the left is the vote — clicking it is how a story climbs the rankings.

1. Look at **the upvote arrow**.
2. Notice **the upvote arrow**.

### 1.5 Story byline

Below each title sits the byline: points, who posted it, when, and a link into the comments.

1. Look at **the story byline row**.
2. Notice **byline: points, user, age, comments**.

### 1.6 Comments link

That comments link is the gateway to the discussion — let's open it.

1. Look at **the comments link**.
2. Notice **the comments link — gateway to the thread**.

## 2. Scene 2: Inside the discussion <a id="2-scene-2-inside-the-discussion"></a>

### 2.1 Open the discussion

1. Click **clicking the comments link on the top story**.

### 2.2 The story header

The story page repeats the title and byline at the top, like a header for the conversation that follows.

1. Look at **the title at the top of the story page**.
2. Notice **the story header section**.

### 2.3 The comment tree

Below the header: the discussion itself — a tree of replies, indented by depth.

1. Look at **the start of the comment tree**.
2. Notice **the whole discussion thread**.

### 2.4 A single comment

Each row is one comment: who wrote it, how long ago, and the text underneath.

1. Look at **a single comment's body**.
2. Notice **one comment: author, age, text**.

### 2.5 Collapse toggle

The minus toggle on the left collapses any subtree — handy for taming the deep threads.

1. Look at **the collapse toggle**.
2. Notice **the minus toggle that collapses a subtree**.
